note: Paired t-tests over 3 seeds (n=3). With n=3, p<0.05 requires very large effects. Cohen's d > 0.8 = large. 95% CI uses t_{2, 0.025} = 4.303.

PSM_ch_drop_vs_baseline.interpretation: Delta +0.013; medium effect (d=0.46) but p=0.465 -- not significant. Well within 1 sigma of baseline noise.

PSM_stjepa_vs_baseline.interpretation: Delta -0.076; large negative effect (d=-2.62); p=0.034. ST-JEPA significantly HURTS PSM. Driven by representational collapse: h_std~0.003, val_loss~0.005 within epoch 1.

SMAP_ch_drop_vs_baseline.comparison: channel-dropout (rate=0.0, i.e. no dropout) vs matched-protocol baseline
SMAP_ch_drop_vs_baseline.interpretation: Best SMAP dropout rate is 0.0 (no dropout wins). Delta +0.044 appears large but p=0.566. The wide baseline CI (std=0.097) makes this comparison statistically weak -- the 'improvement' is reshuffling of per-seed luck. Channel dropout did nothing for SMAP.

SMAP_stjepa_vs_baseline.interpretation: Delta -0.043; p=0.520, not significant. ST-JEPA collapsed on SMAP but baseline std=0.097 is too wide to detect the regression statistically. Absolute ST-JEPA number (0.489) is near chance; collapse is the real finding.

FD001_ch_drop_vs_baseline.interpretation: Delta +0.011; small effect (d=0.20); p=0.803. Channel dropout has no meaningful effect on FD001.

FD001_stjepa_vs_baseline.interpretation: Delta -0.227; very large negative effect (d=-4.05); p=0.020. ST-JEPA significantly HURTS FD001. Collapse confirmed. Note: baseline is already 0.065 below v30 reference (0.721 vs 0.786), so the absolute ST-JEPA number 0.494 is even worse than the delta suggests.
